qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via unbounded $regex query with no default maxTimeMS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unvalidated MongoDB $regex query execution causing denial of service, while the advisory root cause is a redirectClassNameForKey authorization bypass that exposes protected session-token data.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Parse Server redirectClassNameForKey query-redirect authorization bypass or the resulting unauthorized session token exfiltration path.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-6r2j-cxgf-495f describes session token exfiltration through redirectClassNameForKey, requiring redirected queries to receive the same class-access checks as direct queries.
dos-unbounded-regex/vulnerability_report.json identifies a separate $regex denial-of-service issue involving MongoTransform new RegExp(atom.$regex) and missing default maxTimeMS.
The finding's affected entrypoints, exploit path, and impact do not mention redirectClassNameForKey, query redirection into a protected class, class-access check bypass, or session token disclosure.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was reported, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
